title: it does not deliver the capabilities it promised
text: this product promoted itself by making what i can only characterize as deceptive promises about the product's capabilities this product was advertised as having support for high speed ddr4 ram they featured a bios program i e d o c p that would configure the bios to run the ram at it's rated speed and timings this software consistently failed producing a black screen that would not post requiring a hard boot by resetting the cmos to factory settings even to reenter the uefi bios very inconvenient the ram was rated at 3600mhz and cas latency 16 but i was only able to consistently run it on this motherboard with any stability at the jedec default of 2133 mhz i experience this with two different sets of ram so don't blame the ram manufacturers i believe that this is a problem with the motherboards timing circuits which appear to be very unforgiving this experience left me very disenchanted with this motherboard on top of this they provide very little support to customers that were experiencing this problem relying on customer forums who members repeatedly implied that you bought products at your own risk what kind of customer service is that br br i would advised shoppers to be very leery of this product i suggest that other shoppers go online to check out other user's complaints

title: output is good but it really needs an input gain control
text: first impressions of the unit are pretty good setup is painless there is virtually none it appears to be well built and the audio output is very good though at best only a minor improvement over the onboard audio on my surface pro your mileage may vary but onboard audio is pretty good these days so don't expect huge leaps in audio fidelity here having said that you'll likely get access to higher bit depths and sample rates than before if you care about such things so there's that i've read a number of complaints about the volume knob crackling when turning it but that's easily fixed with some contact cleaner there are also a number of complaints about it just going bad or otherwise breaking so only time will tell br br the bigger issue for me is the audio input it's not necessarily bad in terms of the audio quality but they added a rather aggressive compression circuit or automatic gain control as they call it and set the mic gain to a fixed level compression isn't necessarily a bad thing as you would typically want to add some anyway and it works fine if you happen to have a similar mic to what they seem to have designed the circuit for aka an antlion modmic but if you're using a different louder mic it will be a noticeable downgrade over almost any other option in this case the lower the output of your mic the better off you will be the closer the mic's sensitivity is to about 35 db the better the real shame here is that all they had to do was put a gain knob on the box and it would've been perfect this would allow you to control not only the mic gain but also the amount of compression since changing the gain effectively changes the compressor's threshold it may be possible to modify the fulla to add a gain control but as is it's a real missed opportunity br br examples of usable mics based on sensitivity specs i have not tried them all br antlion modmic uni or any previous wired modmic br hyperx cloud series maybe br steelseries arctis series maybe br br examples of unusable mics br any razer headset br logitech g pro series and possibly other logitech headsets

title: physical install is easier than programming instructions are hopeless but competitor videos help
text: i ve always wanted an evil lair just like any of james bond s nemeses or is the nemesi what s the plural of nemesis anyway i ve always wanted an evil lair just like one of james bond s enemies and you can t say you have an evil lair without a high tech bio metric access system so when i saw this fingerprint door lock i jumped insert evil laugh here br br it isn t as clean an install as you might like i think the strike plate is designed for exterior doors it s too big for interior doors if you're installing this inside the house as i did you can either enlarge the strike plate area on the door frame this involves a chisel hammer paint and some skill or you can just buy a replacement strike plate in the same finish for 0 29 at the hardware store though i m fully capable of the former the latter is just so much easier i m using this door handle to replace a cheap builder grade brass door handle the rosettes on both the outside and inside are just slightly smaller than the rosettes they replace so i may need to touch up the paint br br the manual is long on the physical installation but ridiculously short on programming the one physical installation issue where they need help is with installing the circuit board plugging the circuitry within the inside handle into the wire from the outside handle isn t really intuitive until you figure out that each connector has a red spot on it line up the red spots and it works but of course they don t explain that in the manual when it s properly connected it will beep three times another thing they don t explain in the manual oh by the way all of the audio commands were in i assume chinese so unless you understand spoken chinese they re not going to be much help there is a code to change the prompt language that s in the manual buried towards the back br br i still haven't been able to pair the door handle to bluetooth there is a trick to getting the handle to enter bluetooth pairing mode but i haven t yet figured out what that trick is i sent emails to the manufacturer to ask for support we ll see how that works but i haven t yet received a reply br br for a while any fingerprint could open the door so much for evil lair i was however able to program one fingerprint i figured that out by get this watching a competitor s video it turns out the procedure is the same to add the first admin fingerprint br long press the setting button the hole on the inside handle with the screwdriver for 2 seconds the blue light flashes br press the fingerprint reader button five times with the finger you want to register blue and green light flash alternately and you ll hear a command after each press br when the green light goes on solid the fingerprint is registered and you re done br unfortunately the competitors manuals and videos are no further help i'm still hoping to pair with bluetooth if i figure that out i'll update this review

text: i use a lot of trail cameras and have been looking for an alternative to trees straps and bungee cords i do not use screw into tree type of mount i've been testing hme t post holder which is also a great product at a very reasonable price around 10 but t posts are not nearly as convenient to use as this product which is a post and holder in one i haven't been using it long but so far it's the best for my purposes my ground is not very hard so pushing into the ground was not tough it is a telescoping pole with a maximun height of 36 which is what i used and worked well since it only goes into the ground about 8 inches i was concerned it might not be sturdy enough no worries it's just fine easy to take up but sturdy enough to do the job br contrary to some reports the swivel arm goes side to side and up and down at first i also thought it only went up and down but soon realized this is simply because the nuts that control the tension were a bit tight use a little more elbow grease or loosen the nut br i just started using this mount so i have no experience yet with the rust issue other than to say a little rust isn't too bad unless it significantly reduced the life expectancy of the product if i get 3 4 years out of a 21 product it will do until something better comes along in light of these comments i think i'll spray a bit of wd40 on the unpainted parts br ignore the complaints about the packing while perhaps somewhat justified who cares finding something that's this well suited for the job at a reasonable price and them complaining about some tough plastic that a pair of scissors will easily cut is a waste of time and an unfair criticism of the product itself br the camera mounting bolt could be a little longer but still works fine on my browning the mount came with four rubber washers i removed three to allow more thread engagement i'm buying a 4 pack of these mounts today when they arrive i'll see if the bolt can be replaced if not i'll still be a happy user br note i use this type of unsecured camera mount on the interior of my property where my property adjoins public use lands i recommend something with a locking mechanism after they've been in the ground for a year or so t posts can be tough to get out when driven sufficiently into the ground i'd recommend a 6' post driven nearly 3' deep

title: the lights are on but no one is home
text: man where to even start you might want to get get snacks for this i own one of the steinberg products that requires this device they do not allow use the soft e licenser with certain software products so i fire up my software a few weeks ago and it informs me that i do not have a current license on this computer huh that's odd i check the elicenser manager software and the usb elicenser is not showing up i follow their extensive troubleshooting up to and including deleting dll files deep within program file folders restart uninstall restart reinstall the elicenser manager software as per their instructions several times nothing try the usb elicenser on a different computer with a fresh install of the elicenser manager software still just not being even registered as a usb plug event it still lights up but it is definitely unresponsive so i immediately request a replacement which takes an additional nine days to show up then the real fun starts you are not able to just transfer a license back off of a usb elicenser back to steinbergs servers and on to a different elicenser you instead 'request steinberg zero downtime' which cancels and deactivates the broken elicenser completely and you are then issued a temporary 25 hour license for the product which is then loaded on to the new elicenser this is where it really gets exciting to get your permanent license back they email you a pdf form that you have to fill out print and sign and date affirming that you confirm that the elicenser in question is indeed defective and unusable lucky for me i have a pdf editor a printer and a suitable camera you then must scan or photograph the document and email it back to steinberg support to be approved for the license to use the product that you have paid in full for some time prior i am currently still waiting to hear back to see if i am approved to keep using the product that i paid several hundreds of dollars for in the mean time i am unable to offer certain services to clients that are contingent on the use of the software product in question due to the sudden and inexplicable failure of the elicenser not cool man any downtime in the studio costs money and we are at two weeks and counting so all in all a terrible experience so far hopefully we can get back up and running as i continue to enjoy using steinberg software its great when it works but there exists a serious reliability issue with the elicenser a completely unnecessary potential point of failure that you will pay above and beyond the original software cost for the privilege of using anyway thanks for coming to my amazon review

title: ditch the 7200 rpm hdd and this is a 5 star desktop but you can easily install a fast nvme m 2 stick and make it fast
text: i'm giving it 4 stars instead of five for the slow hard drive it came with the western digital blue hard drive makes this computer boot up and perform slow msi really should have installed a hybrid hard drive if they didn't want to spend the money to include a full on ssd i will say that the hard drive included is known as being very reliable but this computer has two pci nvme m 2 slots it also comes with a screw kit if you want to add such ssd cards i installed a samsung 950 pro m 2 nvme stick and the computer boots up loads applications and performs stupid fast i used samsung data migration software to transfer everything over you'll need to remove the hard drive that came with the pc and the computer will than boot up on the crazy fast m 2 nvme stick br br this desktop uses laptop memory to conserve space laptop memory isn't slower than desktop memory spec for spec although you have less choices to chose from msi says this desktop comes with one 8gb ddr4 2133 sodimm of ram it actually came with kingston ddr4 2400 ram and one slot available to add more another discovery in the bios is that it comes with a killer branded ethernet it has built in intel wi fi ac and blu tooth 4 2 which is nice with the cover removed there is a slot for a 2 5 34 ssd hdd on the top and there is space for an additional 3 5 34 desktop hard drive next to the one that came with it it's crazy that there is space for five hard drives on this pc 2 5 34 2 3 5 34 's and two nvme m 2 slots br br i really really like the small form factor of this desktop and is why i chose it the desktop is 9 34 5 8 inches tall 13 34 3 4 deep and 5 34 wide br it is also quiet you can hear it but it's quieter than previous desktops i've had br the nahimic audio manager gets a 5 5 i've never had better sound come out of my koss portapro headphones

title: worked well with d cells would hold only 3 of my aaa's and went haywire after 2 weeks
text: i needed a charger that would charge aaa through d cell batteries many of the advertised features on this product attracted my attention especially the smart charging features with automatic shutoff i own several tenergy batteries and they work fine so i decided to try their charger it looked like a good deal for the features it had br br when i received the charger it felt a little cheap but managed to charge my 4 d cell batteries just fine the led graph for each cell shows the charge state of each battery and when charging is finished the charger automatically shuts off so far so good i next tried charging my aaa batteries i could only get the charger to hold 3 of the aaa cells the fourth cell fit too loosely to make connection with the contacts i didn't have this problem with my aa cells and charging worked fine br br after two weeks my d batteries needed recharging this is where i ran into another problem after charging the d cells i noticed they were much warmer than the last time i charged them i also noticed the led charge status screen was blinking on and off which it hadn't done before i took out the batteries to see if the screen would stop blinking even with the batteries out the screen continued to show all batteries were still charging not only this the screen showed that the discharge cycle was on and that nine volt batteries were being charged along with my 1 5 volt batteries something in the circuitry had apparently fried resulting in an over charging of batteries and cuckoo charge status graphics br br i ended up contacting amazon and they said i had about a month to return the charger and get a full refund instead of taking my chances with tenergy again i ended up spending nearly twice the money on a german made charger the ansmann energy 8 plus asin b003v89rys ansmann energy 5207442 us 8 plus battery charger and maintenance device br br i delayed sending back the tenergy charger so i could compare it to the ansmann charger i found the overall quality of the ansmann better than the tenergy charger having a stout plastic case made of thicker plastic and a power cord made of much higher gauge wire the ansmann could also charge 6 aaa or aa cells while the tenergy was limited to a maximum of four cells the batteries fit easily into the ansmann charger bay with no loose connections br br i have now charged multiples sets of batteries from aaa to d cells with my ansmann energy 8 plus without a hitch while the quality of the ansmann is higher than the tenergy it is missing 3 features found on the tenergy unit a discharge mode a usb port and cover over the battery bay br br if the tenergy charger had continued to work like it did when i first got it i would have given it a rating of four stars down grading it one star for not being able to charge all four of my aaa batteries since the charger failed after only two weeks of use nearly destroying four of my d cell batteries i can only give it a two star rating

text: you're going to need some massive hands and an enduring grip to make use of this mouse br br for reference i'm 6'1 and prefer a larger mouse to fit my large hand but i struggle to consistently click the left mouse button due to the pressure required if pressed anywhere but the far front of the button the right clicks fine no issues there the scroll wheel is already making an unhealthy grinding sound and i've only been using it since just after christmas 2022 see date of this review for time frame not long br br as far as accuracy and consistency it's very good as you'd expect from anker it has a good range i can take it with me to the kitchen to make a coffee and change up music or video on my laptop which is about thirty feet or so it doesn't require any manual install or downloading drivers like some of the other wireless mice i've used br br overall were this a mouse that didn't require constant pistol like gripping to use no real texture ridges or anything to grasp so you have to squeeze it to keep your hand from sliding off and didn't show signs of the scroll wheel failing after only a couple of weeks i'd give it a shrug and call it average br br it's a gimmick that sounds good on paper but in practice leaves my hand wrist and forearm much more strained than my old utechsmart venus that this was potentially to replace i couldn't recommend it even to people with monstrous hands and herculean endurance since you could just get a different mouse and be just as well off br br i'd also highly recommend against getting this for gaming especially competitive gaming it's accurate and fast but the excessive strain of gripping it like a pistol which sounds cool admittedly will wear your limb out fast and trying to relax leaves your hand slipping down onto the table desk where it drags and slows down your response time as well as leading to difficulty getting the right button to click br br sorry anker i usually love your stuff but this mouse is getting refunded i think

title: decent player if you learn its secrets
text: this is my first bluray player and i got it because i have an aquos display i ignored a cryptic error message for several weeks until i realized that i was watching 480p even on bluray discs br br secret 1 there is a setup sequence where you have to hold down one of the buttons on the remote for two seconds in order to tell it to display at a high resolution i did not expect to have to do this when i have an aquos display they should recognize each other right the setting appears to be sticky as i now enjoy hd resolution without having to perform this unusual setup sequence each time br br like others on this forum i had trouble watching avatar at first i tried using the usb drives i had lying around to do the upgrade but it would not mount them even after reformatting them br br secret 2 use the usb drive that comes with the unit to transfer the firmware image thanks to others on this forum for revealing this secret br br with the new firmware installed last night avatar played fine br br i find the unit's responsiveness to be on the slow side for a consumer electronics product operating from either the front panel or the remote i often have to press the open button multiple times when it first powers up i would expect to be able to press power then open but in my experience you have to wait a good ten seconds before the open button responds br br when loading discs it often takes a while tens of seconds to get past the loading sequence not counting the annoying trailers and commercials for bluray technology that it tries to force you to watch once loaded the unit responds reasonably quickly to commands and i've never had a problem with the popup menu pausing during playback is responsive but expect two seconds or so when resuming br br i did have trouble playing one set of audio cd's but i don't play a lot of audio cd's so i don't know how widespread this problem is maybe i'll try again now that i've got the latest firmware

title: cheap remedy
text: if you own an ipad you probably already know what the principle flaws of the device are and at least three of them relate to the screen for one it's extremely reflective which can be distracting and annoying especially if you're outside or in direct light of any kind secondly it picks up thumbprints faster than gil grissom at a forensics contest and doesn't wipe down all that easily lastly while the screen is clearly designed to withstand abuse it has no built in protection this was probably a deliberate and cynical move on apple's part to force you to buy an expensive leather carrying case br br the natural view screen protector was designed by 3m to address some of these issues in a way that was easy and not very expensive it came with two plastic screen covers a sort of small hand squeegee of heavy cardboard and a wipe cloth the idea is very simple you lay the plastic screen cover over the ipad screen smooth the air bubbles out with the squegee and then remove fingerprints with the wipe cloth as they accumulate does it work br br well the protector does nothing or very little to cut down on screen glare which is too bad because this is one of the most annoying features of the ipad however it doesn't really make the claim to do this so i can't harp on that fact it does protect the screen quite nicely and the wipe cloth is a dream to use a couple of quick once overs and it's clear as crystal the principle trouble is largely with the way the squeegee works or doesn't work on the cover if you are a ninja and can lay down the cover perfectly i suppose you'll hardly need the squeegee to push the air bubbles to the margin of the screen if you lack ninja skills or perhaps have the ninja skills of chris farley ahem you will spend a lot of time trying to scrape those little away and even then there may be a tiny bubble or two that just won't move what's more even the smallest speck of dust on the actual screen will show as a tiny white dot when the cover is laid down over it so you have to polish the sucker spotless before you apply br br i had trouble deciding whether to give this product three or four stars and i reluctantly settled for four mainly on the strength of the wipe cloth which is fabulous because you get two covers instead of just oen and because it's overall an economical way to protect the device i would however recommend that anyone using it make sure the screen is spotless and take great care to lay it down flat so as to minimize the time spent chasing air bubbles with the squeegee

text: i'm making this review with hopes for an issue for this headset to be solved although after searching the forums it seems that br logitech isn't going to do much about it br br this headset is a cosmetic oil smears from the hands br three shortcut buttons on the right side of the headset useful for speaking on push to talk applications when away from the computer br slightly flexible replacements from logitech i have also lived in two separate households during the two years i have owned this headset br br the product is given 1 star solely because of its inability to produce title: be sure to update firmware first
text: despite a good number of not so good reviews i went for this stbn100 nas anyway historically i've had pretty good luck with seagate so i leaned in the direction of getting a nas from seagate i got it installed two 3tb hard drives and put it on my network at first i had trouble initializing the second drive it just wouldn't let me no matter what i did then i remembered that i should check for firmware updates br i found updated firmware and installed it it was pretty easy you need two different files br after the firmware update most functions started working correctly i set up shared folders for my music pictures backups and family members i was able to add users and groups and configure access to each shared folder the way i wanted it's a really sweet server br next i set up some directories for the private cloud services to access some of my folders from the internet this is where my experience went sour seagate uses a service called tappin for global access of the files directions are murky setup is clunky it only half works and what does work is really slow i attempted to share my music and pictures folders for remote access i was able to access the music folder although at very low rates i couldn't access the pictures folder to save my life i even turned off sharing on the music folder in the hope that the picture folder would show up somehow i was still able to access the unshared music folder and still not see the pictures folder what's up with that br to add insult to injury the tappin service is a pay for service after you use your initial three free years maybe they'll get it working in three years for now i'm shutting down my near worthless private cloud br still the stbn100 is a great local server for cloud services it is not ready for consumers br br see my updates below added jan 5 2014

title: decent for the price but if it had cost more i'd return it
text: as far as wireless mice go this one is ok specifically because it is inexpensive the battery seems to last forever it tracks well feels decent in the hand and seems like an ok all around mouse so long as you have a somewhat light touch and are not doing anything that requires a high level of accuracy or control like cad or photoshop but as a person with large and fairly heavy hands and who does ecad and mcad for a living i have two complaints trying to use this mouse br br first the thumb button on the left side of the mouse seems to have no resistance to it what so ever as a result i find myself inadvertently pressing it just moving the mouse which preforms the 34 back 34 function in windows for most programs this is not a problem as it doesn't do anything but while browsing the web it is a bit of an annoyance br br secondly the wheel is way too sensitive i do a lot of cad work where the wheel is used to zoom in zoom out and the wheel click button middle click is used to pan the view and move around a drawing what i didn't realize when i bought this mouse was that it had 34 side scrolling 34 meaning that you can push the wheel left or right and it will 34 click 34 just like when you push down on it and it will perform a left or right scroll the problem is that unless you are in a document spreadsheet webpage where the window can be 34 scrolled 34 left and right this feature does nothing it takes far less force to side scroll than to do a middle click and in fact requires almost no movement left or right and practically no force to get a side scroll click and once a side scroll is clicked it won't recognize the middle click on top of that as i said rolling the wheel is used to do a zoom in or out depending on which way you roll and it seems that no matter how hard i try to avoid it because you can't even feel the wheel tick as you roll it i constantly get multiple scrolls on the wheel which zooms several levels at a time by barely moving the wheel a little this means when i intend to only zoom out by one level which is very normal to only be changing the zoom by one level at any given time it usually takes me from where ever i'm currently at on what i'm working on and either zooms all the way out or all the way in making getting it to zoom in or our one or two levels at a time quite a challenge then couple that with the sensitivity of the side scroll and it make the mouse a true hassle to use for cad work fortunately my current project i am working on is a programming application and for that this mouse will work going forward though i am going to replace this with a different mouse for doing my work

title: good at what its for reading ebooks
text: i've compared several different readers mainly the different sony readers and of course the kindle the main reasons on deciding on the sony pocket reader is the more open format its solid price point and its ability to read library ebooks if your local library offers ebooks comparing the different sony readers namely the touch and the daily edition i initially had planned on the sony touch edition because i thought the larger screen size would be nicer and the six inch screen is the same as the kindle 2 however after fiddling with the touch edition at the sony store it is clear that the touch screen has considerably more glare due to the touchscreen compared with the pocket edition i decided since i'm mainly using this as a device to read books not to take notes or find the definition of words in the text that it made more sense to get the pocket edition which screen size is fine at 5 especially when considering the pocket edition is half the price of the touch edition i also looked at the sony daily edition which touch screen is better than the touch edition although i didn't really like the leather backing of it the pricing also was a big factor because i don't really need the 3g connection since i don't really mind just connecting it to the computer to download the books need to do this anyways to charge it yes it would be convenient if you travel a lot to be able to get a book anytime you want but try to think how many books 200 dollars could buy that's about how much you would save so this is a great ebook reader not so great for technical manuals or for taking notes for those purposes the larger screen of the kindle dx or the touch capabilities of the higher end sony's would probably be better i'm happy with it as my first ebook reader and with all newer technology devices a much better one at a lower price will probably be available within 2 years at which point i will upgrade another reason not to break the bank the first time out br br on another note i have read lots of bad reviews of the sony software for managing the ebook library i only use it for books i would buy at the sony store otherwise i use the great calibre ebook manager which is free and is pretty easy to use br br pros br good at what its for reading books br open format br solidly built device br good price br cons br slightly smaller screen than kindle 2 and higher end sony's br less frills no memory card reader or audio connection if these are necessary br no 3g connectivity expected at the pricepoint

title: the nreal air is not ready for prime time
text: i guess i fell for the hype and believed the army of youtube promoters this is going to be an honest but critical review from a person who was originally very excited about the potential of these glasses br br 1 the left lens on my pair of glasses was incredibly blurry i confirmed it was the lens and not my left eye i have 20 20 vision by simply turning the glasses upside down and looking through the blurry lens with my right eye i confirmed the results again by having friends and family test it out they all found the left lens to be blurrier br br i have read other accounts of this and it s starting to look like nreal may have a quality control issue with these lenses br br 2 the clarity even on my functional right lens color reproduction and dynamic range of this display is really just disappointing nothing close to any oled tv you ve seen monitor or even the low res 720p oled nintendo switch display sure the display might be better than a quest2 in one or two ways but i found that the edges of the nreal air display were always so blurry and distorted that my eyes hurt within minutes of attempting to watch or read anything reading text is definitely the worst some scenes in videos are passable the lack of dynamic range is so bad that blacks look totally crushed and highlights totally clipped basically the display is the opposite of hdr and not even sdr it s more like ldr if you are mirroring onto the nreal air with a high quality device like i tried with an ipad pro an m1 max and an iphone 14 pro you will catch yourself wanting to look down and look at the vastly superior device you are mirroring from not the inferior nreal air br br 3 the nebula ar space support on m1 macs at least is a total joke i did enjoy being able to have 2 separate displays to throw up full screen content alongside my mac the third screen is always a mirror of your real display but again the quality and clarity was so horrendous that i ended up preferring to use literally any display over the nreal air s br br 5 there s a halo effect that surrounds the screen you can see it even in a well lit room but in the dark it s even more noticeable basically an illuminated ring that goes around the rectangular display and it s just super distracting and makes the experience feel cheap also if you move you head even slightly during the ar space mode the screens wobble and jitter further reducing the already poor image clarity and viewing experience br br 6 i wanted these to be good so badly i really did they just aren't and the quality control issue with one lens was the nail in the coffin it just feels like a prototype not a device worth close to 500 once you get the adapters you need for ios and game consoles etc the cool factor of having a floating display in front of your face wore off very quickly just imagine how dumb we will all feel having this thing once apple and all the big players release their polished ar vr headsets this year br br some good things br br 1 the build quality is good enough for the price and comfortable enough to wear for maybe 30 min 1 hour before feeling discomfort around the nose and forehead br 2 i like that it runs of usb c power delivery from the host device because it keeps it light and having to charge this thing would have been an enormous pain the tiny battery would have lasted for like 30 minutes br br in conclusion i strongly recommend skipping this device and waiting for one of the many more desirable options we will have in the next few months

title: works some of the time
text: i've used the virgin mobile mifi since september 2010 i bought it the day after david pogue wrote about the 40 unlimited plan at br br good i can be online most anywhere for example i will often boot up my laptop and work while on the bus br br bad the connection is unreliable sometimes the mifi will work fine all day but on too many days it disconnects every few minutes and won't reconnect until i turn it off and on again or restart it through the software on its bad days virgin mobile mifi service is far worse than dialup br br i contacted customer service by twitter phone and email the reps are kind and patient but not trained they can tell you how to set up the device and how to reset it but that's all if that doesn't fix the problem they promise the investigative team will call you but they never do after many calls customer service sent me a replacement mifi device virgin mobile even omitted part of my address on the package but the diligent fedex man found me anyway the replacement behaves exactly the same on bad days it disconnects every few minutes br br because some people like the mifi my guess is that network reliability varies by location so depending where you are virgin mobile broadband may work fine for you if you get lucky buying the mifi device and paying 40 50 per month is a good deal but as it so often doesn't work in my neighborhood i'm about to abandon virgin mobile br br speed when connected i've never seen the mifi download much faster that 1 mbps on workday afternoons 300 kbps is more typical that's still fast enough for me br br one more thing about virgin mobile they don't report system status anywhere they've had a few widespread network outages when those happen if i browse to a web site the browser completes the dns lookup but its status just sits at waiting virgin mobile could avert lots of irate calls and tweets by just putting a message on their web site and on twitter to say they're having an outage and expect to be back at whatever time they never do this

text: i had a situation where i needed to setup a usb hub about 50 feet away from a home theater pc on an 87in tv we have a huge gathering room that opens to a dining room and kitchen and the dining room table and kitchen are outside of the range of our logitech unifying device transceivers we also wanted the ability to plug in other usb devices about 50 feet away from the physical pc so we picked up this pair of boxes and got a 50ft flat ethernet cable that we could run around the edge of the room a single ethernet cable is much flatter than one of those massive active usb cables with repeaters built in ever 25ft to boost the signals this worked perfectly for our break out needs and by the time you add the price of a good hub to an active cable that long and the ac dapater which is not included in most active usb cables over 50ft this was a better value and it's rock solid

title: not the sharpest buy at least for me
text: i wish i could say this is a great tv br br pros it has a terrific picture is roku ready which makes it a better streaming tv than most smart tvs and has all the calibration options screen modes you will ever need its full array led back lighting is much better than the edge lighting used in other tvs in its class too despite a terrible remote tiny keys no universal capability for other devices and a laughable favorite channel design the tv is a great buy on paper and when it works as it should it's a pleasure to watch i assume anyone getting an led uses a sound bar or a sound system so let's forget about the audio br br cons the problem with this model is its lack of reliability as many others have pointed out i discounted this when i bought the tv thinking that the price was right through amazon and that the possibility of recurrent failure was slim statistics i thought were on my side br br well before it was 2 months old my tv's mhl roku port failed after 2 3 weeks of waiting for a new motherboard the replacement motherboard's hdcp failed as soon as the board was installed so with all the hdmi mhl ports fried the tv could only display video through its component cables or basic cable antenna set up this meant no roku no bluray no digital cablebox after scheduling 3 disruptive service appointments my tv was eventually determined by sharp's technical group to have a 34 no fix 34 issue br br though i have finally reached an equitable agreement with sharp on an acceptable replacement to be delivered within the next 3 weeks or so making the time that i will have had this faulty tv actually longer than the amount of time it worked properly owning this tv and the process of resolving issues with sharp is an exercise in aggravation and frustration i wouldn't wish on anyone br br so my advice to 34 anyone 34 who decides to buy this model would be to definitely get an extended warranty don't be surprised by port failures and other design issues pray a lot and retain a therapist

title: led apple to recommend against their use keyboard covers since they might damage
text: moshi keyboard covers are uniquely made of ultrathin 1 mm thick smooth feeling thermoplastic urethane whereas every other brand keyboard cover is made of thick somewhat cloudy slightly sticky silicone that because of their thickness led apple to recommend against their use since they might damage mac laptops with retina displays due to the limited space furthermore after reading product descriptions and customer reviews for nearly every brand of keyboard covers including kuzy topcase and uppercase covers i found that the greatest criticism was that they all didn't feel as comfortable as the uncovered keyboard my wife had a kuzy brand cover and while it fit my laptop's keyboard it was thick somewhat cloudy very uncomfortable with the silicone rubber feeling textured in a way that the fingers can't slide easily this was not the case with the moshi cover it is so thin smooth and dry feeling that it is close to not having anything on the keys i'm frankly astonished that moshi doesn't trumpet the virtues of their unique keyboard cover's feel but they don't the cost at 24 95 is a little pricey but compared to all other products it's absolutely completely worth every penny and because the moshi is so thin i'm confident that it will not press against the retina display screen i also just recently purchased a thule vectros case for the same macbook pro and i'm very impressed with it so far no other case seems so beefy and well engineered it has a unique rubbery impact liner beneath the cover that should provide plenty of protection while i'm on a long road trip through alaska and canada there's no way that it could possibly slip out of my hands given the large gripping surfaces with this case it looks a little industrial but its utilitarian and rugged appearance has its own unique esthetic

title: works great audio out
text: 4 30 22 update still going strong i've since switched to the sony xperia 1 iii so i no longer use this for my phone but occasionally use it on my samsung galaxy tab s8 if i want to use my wired headphones no wear on the connector or cable the reinforced joint where the cable and the plugs meet are working as designed br br using this with my one plus 7 pro using the uapp app it does recognize it as a 32bit 384khz dac same goes for my windows 10 pc i can see the audio sampling to this dacs specifications and it plays any audio without a hitch br br i like the design of it comparing this to my other usb c dac adapter adv sound's accessory lite i do like this design when plugged into my phone it feels sturdy the intertwined cable is reinforced at each end by some hard plastic so it seems like it'll survive multiple bends the cable is also wrapped in what feels like rubber or plastic the ends are made of what feels like metal and plugging a set of headphones in creates a satisfying click and lock br br my headphones range from whatever mood i'm in if i want to listen to a more reference signature i go for my beyerdynamic dt990 pro 250ohm with the dac they're a bit brighter at the high end bass is clean mids are crisp highs are very crisp the dac is able to drive these ok but i do have to raise the volume up to 50 70 with lower impedance headphones like my onkyo e700m they can drive them at comfortable levels around 25 30 volume driving my planar magnetic hifiman he4xx is similar to trying to drive my bd headphones they require a bit more power to perform br br one thing of note product description says you can use your headphone's calling functionality buttons etc but using my onkyo headphones the button does not work it will not play pause skip or function as an answer hang up button it could just be my unit or even my headphones but so far with one pair the functionality does not work br br for my phone audio is clear crisp and clean i mainly listen to metal with some edm some video game soundtracks with some days where i feel like listening to american oldies my main library of personal flac files sound awesome br br although from a typical consumer me stand point these work just fine you're not going to hear much of a difference between these and say google's usb c to 3 5mm adapter also unless you have the proper equipment and super human hearing to be able to experience 384khz sample rates it's a bit overkill if you're really going to require that sort of audio out capabilities you're not going to look for a sub 100 dongle just saying br br overall this adapter does work my phone and pc recognize it as a usb dac capable of the advertised audio sampling and bit rate

title: use may result in networking problems
text: a older version 2 0 of this product is being sold for almost the same price br br asin b00t76thz2 mirabox hdmi extender over ip tcp cat5 5e 6 6e rj45 utp stp ethernet lan switch network support 400ft 120m 1080p full hd extension black hsv373 a pair br br the product on this page is version 3 0 and it syncs more reliably than 2 0 br br neither of the products work very well if you put them on your network i spent hours trying different configurations on my home network and i was amazed at some of the havoc caused the most interesting thing was the specific ability to complete disable router wifi transmission i was able to do this to my xfinity gateway as well as two different netgear routers x6 and x8 this happened with repeated experimentation and the wifi broadcasting returned when i removed the extender from the network br br even though the device works it fails for me because at times it renders my network useless br br it actually works just fine if you want to use cat5e or 6 cable to connect the transmitter and receiver directly br br i also works pretty well if you connect it to an un managed switch rather than directly to a router i don't know enough about networking to understand why this was case br br my goal was to connect to it a router or even a switch connected to the router on one end and a high quality multi band wifi extender on the other end set up as non broadcasting wireless access point and placed in reasonably close proximity to the router doing this resulted in a signal that constantly went in and out 85 out with 15 flashes of low quality picture despite measuring internet download speeds of 62 100mbps from the ethernet port of the access point clearly though i didn't have enough bandwidth to receive the transmitted hdmi signal

title: boss engineers have bested physics
text: alright guys let's do some math remember this is assuming you have an aftermarket stock high output alternator to find watts all you have to do is multiply volts by amps so the highest watts this amp is capable of producing before it blows all three fuses is w 14 4 this is a very generous voltage for a battery unless is it aftermarket x120 there are 3 40 amp fuses which equals 1728 watts this is the maximum number of watts the amp is capable of producing not sustained but maximum so the supposed rms output of the amp is 1222 watts so unless the engineers over at boss beat the laws of physics this amp cannot produce anywhere near where they claim it does and if you have a stock alternator between 70 100 amps the number of much lower 1 stars for price is being generous take the money you would have spent and go to a better brand

title: mine is working in costa rica
text: thought i'd pop on and write a review because i had some doubts when purchasing this i bought it to spare myself from paying 10 day for 0 5g internet in costa rica costs 20 to do 1 hour of zoom at that rate you sometimes do need a backup here when the wifi goes out all over town i am a digital nomad so having consistent internet is important for me this little device got speeds of about 35 mpbs inside my house in the states before leaving keep in mind your phone will be faster b c it's on 5g in most places in the states and some cities in europe some people writing reviews for these devices didn't seem to consider that but 35 is fast enough for many purposes currently i am in avellanas costa rica near tamarindo there are 2 bars of 4g service inside my hotel room according to the device i went to the trouble of getting a sim card from movistar one of the local providers at a mini market yesterday activating the card was a bit of a pain the clerk put in her name and info to activate it which was nice we had to try two different brands of cards to find one that would activate i don't know how much data i bought but the card was only 2 1000 colones possibly around 3 5g according to the brand whose card didn't work it took me a while to figure out how to fit the card in the slot first time changing a sim myself but i finally got it in and it worked the device recognized the sim and joined the movistar network the device started recording how much data i was using off the sim i joined the network with my phone and did a speed test it was about 7 5 mbps with the glocal device in the window 2 bars this is not blazing fast of course but also not too bad not great speeds for zooming or complex activities but it would clearly be better in a location with more bars overall i'm pleased with this product and glad i went with the touch screen i did not go with the pro version because i didn't need the apps phone for that and didn't want something so fragile this one seems a good compromise i also bought the one with no touch screen but returned it i will use this in cr and mexico to back up my hotel or airbnb internet service for the next few months if you have needs similar to that check this out most locations in cr and mexico don't have 5g so no need or a 5g capable modem in these places yet happy travels

title: a decent piece of hardware but there are some problems
text: i'd just like to say that if you plan on using superdisks this is the way to go because just about every drive out there is pretty terrible but this is the least terrible of them all that being said this drive worked and the software was pretty useful and kudos on the compression scheme that was developed which allowed normal floppies to be compressed to 31 9 mb format while in this drive one of the gripes is the simply idiotic shortness of the usb cable so short that a extension cable was necessary which we all know slows down the travel of data by several hundred miliseconds that sounds like nothing but when you're writing large portions of data it makes a difference this drive is removable and you can switch it between several computers whenever you like also it requires no extra source of power another pro to using it now on with the cons the writer software is moronic to a startling degree programs need to be added to the well just a list of programs to be written why you couldn't just select them in the normal file tree was beyond me for users with anything but mac or windows this is a very small minority like myself porting of the program was necessary an extremely boring and fairly difficult procedure the thing is loud it makes a great deal of buzzing noises and i found myself wanting to leave the room because of it and writing is so very slow i got this mostly to switch data between my desktop s and an older laptop which for some reason wouldn't read burned cds so it gets the job done for the most part but if you can just manage to get a decent cd burner like any plextor then i would stay away from this

title: scam warranty faulty product do not buy
text: i bought this modem a little less than 1 year ago and ever since i purchased it i have had issues initially i wrote them off as issues with my isp the two issues likely stem from the same problem but i often experience bouts of high packet loss usually followed by complete loss of signal the signal will stay lost until i unplug the modem and plug it back in br br it did not occur frequent enough that i thought it could be faulty hardware additionally it's peak covid season so i mean it was impossible to tell what the hell is going on br br however after a year of struggling back and forths with my isp and my own troubleshooting i determined that it was the fault of the modem i called netgear to try and troubleshoot and or get a replacement but surprise surprise this company is a glorified scam center br br in order to make use of the 1 year hardware warranty you have to prove there is a hardware issue according to the support technician i talked to that simply means that all the lights are off and it wont power on they do not care if the modem is powered on but not working what's more is that their technical support warranty is only 90 days and in order to actually continue talking to them or move forward with your warranty ticket you have to have an active technical support warranty br br so they refused to replace or diagnose my modem and tried to scam me into paying for an additional warranty just to see if i could replace this modem br br complete scam br br do not buy

title: was the best deal in 2018 but
text: after seeing a linustechtips video reviewing this tcl tv as one of the best affordable smart tvs on the market at the time in 2018 i bought it for my parents to replace an old panasonic crt we've had for ages the tcl was a great tv and worked splendidly but as of 3 9 2021 the tv has since encountered an issue where the screen is black although the sound still works doing a flashlight test i can still see faint images being displayed on the tv upon further research it seems that at least one of the leds had died which in turn shuts off all the other leds causing the tv to remain dark if you google tcl 55s405 black screen you'll find that a lot of people have encountered this issue within the past 3 years not just pertaining to this exact model but other sizes as well if you are out of warranty your options are to either take it to a repair shop if it's a couple hundred probably not worth it fix it yourself with spare parts or toss it in the trash feels bad mang br br while popular in china and other asian markets tcl is fairly new to the western market having entered in 2014 so in hindsight most people probably haven't experienced a tcl product until recently and thus didn't have a formed opinion on the company besides initial product reviews but now i can say i probably wouldn't buy a tcl product again seems like the days of longevity are gone planned obsolescence sucks

title: did not work put my phone out of order
text: i bought this call blocker to block robo calls on my land line phone it was simple to hook up and i am sure i set it up exactly as described after hooking it up i got a dial tone and so it seemed to be set up and working okay about two hours after setting it up i tried to use my phone and the line was dead i figured my at t service was out of order i called at t and the representative i talked to said they were having service problems in my area and were working diligently to fix it so much for the accuracy of at t certainly not like the old days when you got a human to answer and accurately help you anyway this no service went on for two days and in the back of my mind i figured at t was fixing the problem after the two days with no service i decided to unhook the call blocker and guess what my service immediately worked and was back on line so much for at t and especially the call blocker i had missed an important call while th service was out i have tried 3 different call blockers from amazon not amazon's fault but none of them worked properly and i returned all of them i am frustrated with the robo calls as i get several every day there used to be a federal number you could sign up for to not get marketing calls but for a few years now this is not available anymore there is a number that has replaced the original number where you can sign up but it doesn't stop robo calls i see there are several reviews where the people are satisfied with their call blocker this makes me wonder as it was not true with my service system

text: i bought this remote to replace a failing logitech harmony 880 and decided on the urc r50 based on favorable reviews and the poor lifetime of my harmony remote br br the urc r50 feels nice in the hand the buttons are much easier to find blindly than the harmony's and easier to use if you have bigger fingers however that's the only upside i've found to this remote br br they tout the fact that you don't need to get on the internet to program this remote that is unless you actually want to read a manual or learn how to use the advance features such as the macro function programming this remote was the biggest pita i've had to deal with in a long long time scrolling through multiple semi working codes for each device until you happen to find the proper one is tedious and annoying once you finally get all of the devices entered it's time to hit the internet and actually download a manual to try and learn the proper way to do macros br br i've got a relatively small home theater with an onkyo 706 handling all the audio video processing a sony dlp dvd player cable box and separate xm tuner with the logitech harmony i'd plug the remote into the computer usb port fire up their software and tell it what order i'd like certain tasks done i e turn on tv set to input 1 turn on receiver set to tv turn on cablebox pretty easy and quick to do with the urc you have to input each button press which really is a pain when you have 8 or more inputs on the tv multiple inputs on your receiver etc and don't forget each button input for each level of the menus and submenus on your tv and receiver br br this is getting sent back and i'll go back to the harmony line of remotes they have their downfalls as well but the point of this remote is to make life easier and macros easier to do the urc remote fails miserably in this respect

title: it's ok
text: i don't know what the expectations are with keyboards but here is my experience br br i have never used a mechanical one before this br br pros br it seems typing has less effort on this keyboard compared to other types of keyboards br i make less mistakes on this one br the lighting config is controlled onboard without having to install software br the lights are bright and dimmable to zero and every gradient in between even to extremely low brightness br the 3 lock lights are bright enough to seen in my periphery so i can easily tell whether caps lock is on br br cons br the font size is huge i can probably read this from 30 feet away i am not visually disabled and would prefer a sensible font size br the glyph placement on the keys is really awkward they nearly overlap and look ugly it would be nice if it used a standard layout at any font family br the keys are extremely wobbly i know other reviews say the wobble is low but maybe they're comparing it to other keyboards of the same type i wouldn't know i know from an absolute perspective these keys are really wobbly maybe the other ones are worse but this is still bad br 2 of the keys fn and 7 key cannot be customized in the color layout they don't light up at all all other keys light up it appears to be broken if i return it it will be because of this br these may be low profile again relative to other mechanical keyboards but there is nothing low profile about this from an absolute perspective this is a very tall keyboard i will need to raise my wrist rests somehow br the bottom half of the keys don't light up as much as the top half and the wider keys with wider words light up less on the sides br br minus 1 star for the 2 broken keys that i cannot customize the lighting on and 1 star for the wobbly keys br br if you just need something cheap that is easier to type on this will still work well despite the wobbly key caps i wonder how much easier it will be with sturdy keys though

title: good for the price
text: i bought this watch because i was looking for a smart watch for an active person myself and this one had the best reviews for the price and this watch does what i need and what it says it will with just a few minor exceptions it can be difficult to get the watch and the app to sync up at times i have had to unpair and then pair the watch with my phone again a few times to get it to register in the last month that i've owned it i also don't like how limited the options are for different forms of exercise and i question why swimming was on the list and calisthenics are not are people swimming wearing this watch the battery life is excellent i only have to charge my watch while i bathe and it has never even come close to running short on battery once i figured out how to read the sleep charts in the app the sleep function became a lot more useful but my problem is more likely to be sleep that isn't deep enough rather than insufficient sleep the pedometer steps seem to be fairly accurate but i have measured the distance of my walking running routes with gps and the distance is often underestimated by at least 10 also the heart rate seems to change based on where on my wrist i wear the watch and how tight i have the band and that seems to call into question how accurate it is i do like how customizable the app and the watch are you can choose which notifications you receive how often between which times and also change the watch face display but i would like options for english measurements in more places and not just metric but then again perhaps being forced to convert metric to english measurements over and over again will force me to once and for all learn the system that most of the world uses there is also a blood pressure feature advertised but when i tried to use that it told me i needed another device paired with the watch br br pros fairly accurate has almost every feature i need very customizable long battery life and recharges quickly br cons some inaccuracy for distance only some measurements convert between metric and english measurements and doesn't always pair well with my phone

title: support
text: asus support and warranty are by far sub standard if you ever have a board that is no longer manufactured but still under warranty and you send it in you will get screwed i sent a board in for repair and was told it could not be fixed asus gave me a new board that was a different chip set after receiving the replacement board i had to buy new memory and a cpu after spending the additional money for parts i put the system together only to discover the replacement board did not work at all i called asus gave them the serial number on the box only for them to tell me that is came up as a old board after further investigation and removal of stickers placed on the box and the board i discovered that asus had re branded the board serial number with a new sticker with my discontinued boards serial number after the tech support tried to tell me that asus does not do this type of behavior i had them research my name and they found my case then tech support said that since asus gave me a newer board because mine was discontinued they had re serialized my replacement board and therefore i had no more warranty so basically asus could not fix my discontinued board sent me a different board with a different socket type requiring me to buy a new cpu and memory and then refused to fix the replacement board screwing me out of time and money i have since warned friends family and clients and stop buying asus products by the way to asus tech support i know you read and monitor these posting please don't waste my time with a response asking me to call you your company has already screwed me and i am not interested in calling to listen to anymore of your lies and bs

title: frustratingly not as good as it could be
text: this is a well built little camera with high quality imaging and it's a clever piece of tech it's let down by some iffy software some features which aren't available yet and a fundamental design flaw that ruins its unique selling point br br it's no disaster and perhaps software updates will bump this to a 4 star product later br br the design is nice the build quality solid and the pivot's spent a month or so in the corner of my living room without standing out very good it comes with a power adapter that plugs into a mini or micro i can never remember which is which usb port on its rear the usp of the pivot is that it can rotate its camera 360 degrees to capture everything going on in a room and it's true it does just that but it's bundled with a power cord that's less than 4 feet long unless it's going to sit on the floor this means that the pivot will have to be near a wall socket which defeats the 360 degree feature most of the time the 360 degree swing just showed me a bookcase it might work well on an office conference table with built in sockets in the centre but otherwise prepare to run some long wires br br next up the software the pivot pinged me constantly with alerts because i have three cats in the house it couldn't react fast enough to catch them on camera and i could t find a way to change the settings when my house alarm's motion detector kept getting set off we altered it to ignore air movements indicating a body less than a 15lbs this would be a great feature to add to the pivot false positives are annoying br br ironically we came home from a weekend away to find a mouse on our living room floor we had no idea which cat caught it but thought it'd be a great use for the pivot so we scanned through the weekend's alerts noting with some bemusement that most of them had no attached video merely a set of stills nonetheless we found the first set of stills with the mouse laying on the floor and checked the one previous to that somehow the pivot had not recorded for the 9 hours preceding the arrival of the mouse so we had video of nothing from 1am and a picture of a dead mouse at 10am apparently nothing of interest like a cat carrying in a mouse and playing with it happened during that time the power was on i checked and the software set to be active i can only presume the software made a bad choice regarding what to keep and what to dispose of br br on the plus side the other features helped us out especially the humidity sensor which revealed our house is too dry and we didn't have any trouble setting up the iphone app i'm not convinced about the security of the app however since anyone on our wi fi network could connect to the pivot and view the video the app also has some usability shortcomings but those can be improved with time br br i think the biggest issue is that the 'cloud' feature or as normal people would call it 'web site' is still not online if you don't have the phone you installed the app on there's no way to view the camera feed so a lost phone on vacation would prevent you from seeing anything until you got home and attached to the same wi fi as the pivot br br with time and the delivery of all promised features this could be a four star product i'd also like to have the ability to attached it via usb to a computer and use it as a very fancy webcam until it's more complete i can t say this device is worth 200 especially with so many competitors out there

title: planned obsolescence a badly designed product
text: the sound on my sony tv was not up to par so i decided to buy a sound bar the salesperson at best buy gave me the hard sell on this particular unit because one of his fellow employees had returned it deciding he wanted a better unit that should have been my first clue but the price was right at 259 it was still more than what i could have purchased it for on amazon com and i bought it hooking it up was easy and i thought i was off to a good start then in clicking on the two side speakers i immediately noticed that they didn't fit tightly and sagged to the side a bit i thought oh well i saved some money by buying an open box item then while playing with the remote i realized there were absoulutely no controls on it to lessen the bass beyond their preset which was annoying muffled sounding and much too present the bass is unnecessarily booming and can be extremely annoying while viewing our sony tv i hear clicks and pops coming from the sub bass quite often and each time you change from hd to regular tv you have to keep adjusting the sync via the remote or people's words and their mouth movements fall badly out of sync after about 8 months the wireless sub bass started making awful noises as if something was living inside it banging it with a newspaper or my fist will stop the noise temporarily but something is whirring or rubbing inside that unit which i don't dare take apart this is something out of a bad b movie from the 1950's when tv was still fairly new and we put up with these things in 2010 and the day of technology this should not be happening i attempted to take it back to the store where i purchased it but was told that i should either send it back to the manufacturer pay the store where it was purchased to fix it or junk it because it was sold as an open box item yea an open box item now seems to be more than what other stores were selling it for that i didn't find out until after the fact so i now have a philips sound bar sub bass that clicks pops goes out of sync whirrs growls and is generally a piece of junk no one at philips would help me when i called and no one at best buy would help without charging me for it i don't want to spend the money on a new sound bar so we now avoid using our sony tv because of it i will never buy a philip's product again partially because they wouldn't stand behind it and partially because it's just not well made or well thought out was this planned obsolescence so one would have to replace it or just a crummy product to begin with this was a waste of money and a waste of time br a definite do not buy

title: great hardware terrible firmware
text: to preface this i'm a software engineer and dabble in networking for my home automation needs i have several raspberry pi's several computers some wemo devices hue smart lights ring doorbell nest echo etc all over my house when i get some spare time i like to mess around with these devices to see if i can control things within the house with software that i write br br first let's start with the good it easily has the best range of any router i've ever purchased i actually bought it for that reason i can connect to the internet throughout my house 3000 sq ft and both the front and back yard and even my detached garage it also seems to have lower latency and faster speeds than my old tp link 1750 if you're buying it for gaming and you don't really do any in house diy tech stuff it's a great option however i don't really use it for gaming at all br br so why the two stars you ask the firmware it sucks i cannot get a consistent connection between my devices on my home network for example i have a temperature monitor in my garage on a raspberry pi with a little grafana web server on it i also have a wemo smart plug in the garage with a space heater hooked up to it i use these two devices to make sure the temperature in the garage doesn't drop below 40 f because i also have a mini fridge where i keep my home brew beer and i don't want it to freeze solid the idea is to kick on the space heater when the pi detects that the temp has gotten too low however most of the time the pi cannot talk to the smart plug and i cannot reach the pi unless i ping it incessantly for a second example i have a small pi zero hooked up to a gfi outlet in my basement to monitor if it's tripped or not the outlet also has my sump pump running on it and if it goes out i want to know because if i don't reset it in time i'll get sewage all over my basement this actually happened to me last year for a third example i have a vpn running on a pi so that i can connect to my home network from anywhere i set up port forwarding to do just that and it's never worked like it did on my old router it'll connect for a couple of minutes and then drop out and i have to reconnect and the last example i have is the worst my wife bought this 300 printer a couple months ago because she's a lawyer and has to print all kinds of things all the time to get this printer working we have to unplug it and plug it back in so that we can find it on the network this is extremely frustrating it seems to be available for 30 minutes after boot and then we can't find it at all on the network i have a ton of other examples but by this point i assume you get the gist of what i'm saying br br for my last point i would like to talk about customer support it's doesn't exist i emailed asus and haven't heard back in a week so i'm returning this thing and getting a linksys router i really would have liked this to work because it seems like a nice product but it's just not performing the basic tasks that a router should be able to do like my old 80 router did i cannot condone or justify spending 270 on a router that doesn't do the most basic of networking tasks and for that asus has lost a customer for life

text: i have had these for 7 months and have used them a lot br i am pretty critical when expensive and much hyped products fail to deliver but i have nothing but praise for these headphones and the hype is well deserved br music is mostly apple lossless or 320k mp3 br the alan parson's project supertramp elo the eagles pink floyd stevie nicks blondie bon jovie def leppard scorpions joni mitchell abba neil young eric clapton 10000 maniacs many more artists br i mostly use the headphones with an ipod classic 160gb a pioneer vsx 23txh avr and sometimes with an old dell laptop all of these sources provide marvelous sound quality br many reviewers have stated the 'need' for an amplifier and this almost scared me off these but i have not found that to be the case at all br there is a difference between great sound quality and extremely loud volume levels and only those people that want very loud volume levels will need an amplifier in addition to their portable player br the sound quality is excellent with everything i have plugged these into and in my opinion most adults will be more than satisfied with the volume level an ipod laptop or other portable player will produce br other info my galaxy s3 phone sounds fine but it gets hot if i use the headphones with it and it does not get hot using the earbuds that came with the ipod the ipod does not get hot with the headphones the avr will get these loud enough it could cause hearing damage i would buy these again br these headphones have a fairly flat frequency response that i appreciate for bass lovers simply adjust the eq a little bit in the player saame for those that like the highs to sizzle the frequency response of the headphones is not a defect

title: not for everyone a little over a year in i'm less and less impressed with this product
text: i had been looking at the echo google home etc for a few months before i bought one we're not heavy into technology but of course the novelty was fun it's nice to be able to turn music on with a single command and get some basic basic information without having to stop to find a phone or ipad and search br br we have two dots one in the kitchen and one in the master bath we mostly use them for streaming amazon music the one in the master bath is quite buggy and has been unlinked from my amazon account for a while now this is irritating as i use audible and cannot access my library on this device note i'm sure i can resolve this issue by contacting amazon which i've done before and having them walk me through it i wanted to include info about this in my review to show that it's not always as seamless as it should be br br generally they work fine for pulling up a pre set station toddler time is a frequent one in our house but maybe 30 of the time alexa can't hear our commands or gives us something totally off the wall and not even close to what we were asking for also in the demos they show that you can ask the dot to never play a song again but the one song we hate and have asked her to never play again always pops up so annoying br br more often than not when we are looking for random information her response will be hmm i'm sorry i don't know that we've been getting increasingly frustrated with this device and may soon just go back to our more reliable bluetooth speakers br br and on a pretty disturbing note we've been seeing things pop up on social media based on things we've discussed in the house i know one of the benefits of having a smart device in your home is that it is always listening in case you summon it for information but i do not like that it is always listening if it's gathering enough data to know that my kids broke one of our window candles and suddenly an ad for the replacement bulbs very specific to the brand we have comes up on my instagram i need to know more about exactly what information this thing is taking down about me and my family not to mention where is it stored and how well is it controlled br br i should also note that we've used the dot with tp link outlets and were not thrilled with that option either maybe this is where our tech journey needs to end for now as time goes on this device is less and less useful more frustrating and generally concerning to me

when i first plugged it in and powered on the system all i could hear was a very loud buzzing sound i immediately shut the system down and unplugged it i did a bit of research and determined that it was a fault i then went to troubleshooting the system as per an online help forum recommendation and as i'd suspected instantly it was indeed this cable i did a bit of wiggling and determined that it was at one of the connectors i disconnected it completely and removed the outer part of the connector to find that some of the inner braided shielding material was in contact with the central wire these are not meant to be touching as one is hot or positive and the other is ground or negative indication to me that it was assembled by an inexperienced technician or something along those lines maybe just someone in a hurry but in any case i had to repair a new cable myself and it's fortunate for me that i have some experience with this stuff so i was able to not everyone has the knowledge or ability to and would be stuck with a faulty cable and a return if they so chose it was only around 15 for a 50' cable so i didn't complain much i did reach out to the seller and they did get back to me a few days later they also offered a replacement however after i informed them that i'd already repaired the connector myself there was no further communication from either party so after the repair i can say i'd like to know where to find a more forgiving type of cable like my other one as i don't think this is a true subwoofer cable it's simple coaxial cable like the cable internet providers use with rca connectors instead of coaxial connectors and true audio grade subwoofer cables are something else i haven't nailed that one down yet as i have this working for the time being
