title: connection issues
text: i charged it fully as instructed and then paired it with my android device pixel 7 android version 13 br br few observations br 1 if your app is closed i e if you opened it paired it and then closed it then it gets disconnected from the sensor and will not beep you must remember to not close it this is weird other apps like fitbit are able to connect via bluetooth even when closed so this app should be able to connect as well probably a bug in the app br 2 if you reboot your device you must remember to start the app again if not it's not gonna beep because the phone won't get connected to the sensor again other apps like fitbit are able to handle this case br 3 when i went out of range and then came back it didn't beep it had lost the connection even after shaking the device to make sure it's not in standby mode and waiting for a a minute or so it didn't beep i did this experiment again and it worked fine the second time though br 4 it turns from intermittent beep to constant beep at 2 feet i would prefer it to do that at 1 feet so that i can park tightly this is just my personal preference though and not a real issue with the product the intermittency between the beeps changed at the expected distances as per the manual slow beeps at 4 feet changes to medium beep at 3 feet and then to constant sound at 2 feet and closer br br because of the connection issues with the app i am going to return it i really loved the idea of it solar powered no wires no need of additional screens just use your phone but in practice it needs some improvements i think

title: intermittent loss of connection and constant reduction of speed
text: as mentioned in other reviews this router has a large sticker on it telling you to insert the accompanying cd before installing the router it claims that this will lead you through the setup process for both pc and mac computers this is a lie i run a mac with osx so i can't speak for the pc experience but the folder on the cd that supposedly has the setup instructions for mac doesn't exist in fact the cd has no setup instructions for mac at all strike one however it's not that big a deal i found that inserting the cd is completely unnecessary anyway i connected the router as i would any other and it worked i say that in quotations because i immediately noticed a significant reduction in the speed of my internet connection both on my mac and my wii strike two upon further use of the router i found that if i tried to open multiple tabs when browsing with firefox as i often did with no problem when connected directly to my modem the router would lose its connection to the modem i also experienced the same problem when trying to stream movies on my wii via netflix each time the router lost its connection i was forced to wait a minute or so for the router to reestablish its connection strike three you're outta there i returned the router the next day and after some internet research i ordered asin b00006b9h9 netgear rp614 web safe router with 4 port 10 100 mbps switch my parents have been running the same model for several years and have had no problems in the brief time that i have owned it it has far out performed the d link router it replaced i won't be buying any d link products in the foreseeable future

title: not very impressive
text: boy was this thing a disappointment i bought this thing in order to put it in my laptop carrying bag therefore if the computer ever got stolen i'd know where it was br br initial setup wasn't too bad got everything up and running and it seemed to work fine i set it up to only send me alerts when the bag moved in order to save battery life br br about a couple weeks after installation i was away from the house and decided to test the gps unit using the app on the phone i had been working from home the past couple weeks and therefore hadn't really had a chance to test the thing the app itself is an absolute lemon and feels very 1990ish there's no button to request current location like google maps for example you just hit the little crosshair's looking button on the bottom right of the screen to find current location on this app you have to drag an onscreen circle and drop it into a rectangle in order for the thing to send a command problem is that once i send it a command it never responds br br since i was away from the house i used the app to try and id the current location of the bag no luck every time i requested the position nothing happened the battery level on the app said 70 so i thought it was still good once i got home i looked at the gps puck and the thing wasn't even on the battery had died days ago and i had no idea since the app was still showing 70 there is an option on the app for it to send you a 'low battery alert' once the gps pucks' battery gets too low therefore even if the battery was low i'd still get an alert even if the puck itself hadn't moved for days however i never got any sort of low battery alert br br safe to say any faith i had in this thing was gone after that i would never trust this thing to come through if i ever needed it to find a missing piece of property let alone if you're counting on this thing to keep track of anything more valuable like your kids

title: great headphones for the price
text: i have to say i m quite surprised with these headphones they sound great have good battery life and are built well br br setup initial setup was a breeze as soon as i took out the headphones and powered them on the red and blue indicator came on for me to pair the headphones no messy sequence of having to hold any combination of buttons for a period of time br br sound quality sound quality of these are very good music and movies sounds great on these i used these to watch a couple of action movies and listened to a couple of different genre of music from classical country pop dance and rock voices from movies and shows are clear and the treble from music sounds rich and natural the headphones have nice bass which come off more rich and deep versus too pounds like some other headphones br br features the battery life on these are great i plugged these in initially and these were fully charged under two hours i ve been using these for the past 2 days and have watched a movies a couple of shows listened to podcasts and music for a couple of bourse these still are going strong though it s a little hard to tell how much battery i still have it would be great if these things could announce the estimated battery life remaining but that s just a minor nit pick range wise i m able to walk around my whole house roughly 2000 sqft and these maintain a good connection to my phone the whole time i tested the noise cancellation a bit and i could tell a difference with it blocking out ambient noise but given that i m at home now versus on a plain or a noisy cafe it s hard to tell how well the noise cancelling works it appears to work well enough to block out and dull outside noise br br build quality and comfort these things feel very sturdy the headphones also come with a nice carrying case to keep them secure for wise the bands are secure without being too tight on my head so i can continue to wear these for a long time this is a feature i really appreciate on quality headphones i ve had a variety of sets that range in price from 50 up to 350 and these are close to the premium priced phones for comfort br br overall i m really happy with these i would have never imagined that such affordable headphones could sound this good and felt this good on my head i would definitely recommend these to anyone considering them

title: an ok product with a couple flaws
text: i've had my unit for about 14 months the positives are that it was easy to setup and the three apps that were must haves for me are supported that's amazon prime netflix and youtube it also came with a pandora app which was a nice bonus it has several others such as hulu but i'm not a subscriber to anything else so those don't matter to me it works as expected most of the time but definitely not always the unit has three pretty big deficiencies br br problem one is that when loading the amazon prime app about half of the time it kicks up an error that says the app is too big for the available memory sometimes it works and sometimes not now mind you this app came with the box so this is a sony engineering error it gets worse br br ok so when a computer coughs up memory errors when trying to run an app there are two things you normally try to do enter problems two and three with this machine probably the first thing one does when encountering an insufficient memory error is to free up some memory by deleting some things later you probably add or replace a memory module which one hopes is a member of the sd card family hopefully the microsd format but one can roll with anything just so long as it is commonly available well there is no way to delete apps to free up memory you can only add or subtract the apps it comes with from the main menu that's it you can't delete them and you can't use the network interface or the usb port to add an app that is not included in the machine and there is no way to add memory to the machine br br supposedly the apps can be updated over the net through sony and one would think that a running out of memory error would have generated some updates but nothing is out there and if one does some searching on google one quickly discovers that these memory errors are common to several of the sony blu ray players so it's not just a one time engineering error it is a high level engineering leadership problem in their company so i'm going to start looking for another machine that supports my three apps and which has a more flexible architecture br br it seems that sony products just aren't what they once were

title: solid state hard drive failed after two weeks seller was unresponsive don't buy
text: this computer was terrific at first this was one of dell's best latitude designs my company has been issuing us dell laptops for the past 20 years refreshed every 3 years with a new latitude it was extremely fast quiet ran like a champ no bloatware was installed just a lean version of windows 10 was very happy br br 2 weeks after i received it i went to start up the laptop in the morning got a message indicating the hard drive was not found obviously the hdd had failed i contacted the seller via e mail they were immediately apologetic and recommended i contact the manufacturer i was confused as i thought they had refurbished the laptop not dell br br i replied to the e mail asking them to clarify the warranty with dell i got the automated i have been assigned to your issue reply and no response in over a day mind you they replied within an hour to the first e mail so i replied to my reply to bump it got the automated i have been assigned to your issue reply and stony silence from this vendor br br the easiest solution would be for them to replace the hdd in the laptop and reload windows but they didn't respond br br so i called amazon tech support who were very good they had me go thru a few troubleshooting steps in the dell bios everything was working perfectly except the hdd this confirmed the hdd had failed as i suspected the tech asked me if they wanted the vendor to send another or get a refund i told him of my poor experience and opted for a refund amazon immediately refunded my money e mailed me a prepaid shipping return label last night and i boxed up and dropped off at ups today br br very disappointing this was a great computer and i was looking forward to several years of trouble free usage hdds do fail if electronics fail they usually fail in the first month of usage i don't fault the vendor for that i do fault them for doing nothing in 2 days time to help me

title: powerful little gem
text: i got the early version of this camera in japan sold as the ixy 3000 is and am head over heels in love this replaces an sd800is which was a long time favorite the new one got my attention for the whopping 14 7 mp as i have been using this for taking panoramas on a gigapan rig and more pixels count for much there plus there is the full manual focus and exposure modes and then there was the thrill of getting a new camera a few weeks before it was available in the us br br mine is set up with an 8gb sd hc card which feels infinite br br i've been very happy with the quality and i do a lot of macro shots posting hundreds on my flickr site br br the new quickshot mode is nice to shoot through viewfinder where you want the quickest response time and a bonus is a long overlooked feature an on screen battery level indicator there is a small button where you can instantly go to view mode rather than flipping the shooting mode face focussing is very handy video quality is superb having the camera stitch mode used for getting good overlap for single row panoramas on the presets selection is great and w00t it uses the same nb 5l battery so i now have more spares br br my negatives are minor the black style is slick but the plastic shell feels a little cheap flimsy but the camera is sturdy my biggest gripe is the latch on the battery sd card compartment comes open easily in your bag or your pocket the one on the sd800 never popped open on its own the latch for the usb dock too is odd it is hard to figure out where the hinge part is and i always feel like i am going to do it wrong and break it off br br spinning the lower selection wheel takes some dexterity so setting the aperture and f stops in manual mode is going to take some time to be a smooth process br br i've never bought a camera just because it has a large resolution but i am shooting 4416x3312 better than my first generation digital rebel br br i am in love with this little camera

title: worse experience ever
text: i decided to give these a try since they had a major discount at the time but completely regretted it br br first off the pairing process was horrendous i charged the case and tried pairing with their app but it would not detect the earbuds i eventually tried doing it direct with the bluetooth on my phone and got it paired but the app would still not detect them about 30 minutes later of constant resetting finally got them connected to both the bluetooth on my phone and the app br br next despite having 7 different eartips none stayed securely in my ears and kept loosening and falling out under normal use even the app itself has a fit test which failed 80 of the time but would randomly pass every once in a while despite me staying completely still makes no sense how that even works br br i would then test some spotify youtube and random games just to test the sound quality it was just average but then every few minutes i would get a disconnected notifcation for a random side what the heck then i would also get a random low battery notifications as well i immediately open the app each time to check the battery levels and see that each earbud would randomly fluctuate between 100 and 20 something percent what the heck is going on i keep charging it again for another 2 hours just to be 100 sure its good but it still happens br br opening the app just to check on the earbuds is weird too it always takes 8 to 12 seconds for the app to even connect to the earbuds for me to check any settings at all i have never owned a pair of true wireless earbuds that took that long br br the earbuds themselves are a complete pain to take out of the case due to the slippery nature of the earbuds and strong magnets you can't easily pluck them out of the case one easier method is to pinch on the eartips but that's really not how it should be it was just poor design choice to not allow a more convenient way to remove them br br and finally while most of the industry has already moved with a wireless charging case because of powersharing from phones this bulky case really should have had it built in too it is not a deal breaker but it certainly does make things feel less premium br br i really wanted to like these because i had other razer products too but so many flaws built up that i just couldn't justify keeping them i eventually went with some galaxy buds pro and they just plain work instant pairing easy fit clear sound no headaches

text: tl dr don t buy this pc without pre purchasing a different cpu cooler at that point you re overpaying br br i bought this gaming computer for my father as an early christmas present i saw it on sale on black friday and immediately thought of him and purchased it on paper it sounds like a great deal but after i purchased it i realized that this gaming pc is somewhat flawed br br for starters they include the stock cooler for the cpu normally this wouldn't be an issue but with such high demanding games these days and the heavy utilization on the cpu this fan is not adequate nor are the cases fans capable of dissipating heat quickly enough the cpu reached 99 degrees which is absolutely unacceptable how can you market this gaming computer as a gaming computer if you don't include the adequate hardware to use as a gaming computer we are ordering an all in one liquid cooler and hopefully my dad can install it on his own since he lives on the other side of the country from me br br the price on black friday this computer was 580 that is an incredible deal for a pc of this specification with the addition of the all in one liquid cooler still puts the computer under a 650 range i would not pay more than 650 for this computer due to the limited ram it comes with you can find better pre built systems and a much more affordable price without the amd hardware br br my only other gripe is the 8 gigabytes of ram that is included i wish it came with more but i know that there is a shortage of ram or something currently happening but that s alright br br i wouldn t buy this again super annoyed at cyberpowerpc about this nonsense

title: off to the garage with you
text: since there is such a thing as a crazy cat lady i hereby nominate myself as being a crazy router dude stashed away in my garage are more than a couple boxes with nothing but wireless routers of virtually every brand size shape and potential functionality after filling most of these boxes i decided upon netgear as my brand of choice i've several of their models stowed away for what purpose i do not know sadly this here router is about to join the boxed up brigade and not for a good reason br br i purchased this router a year after i purchased a netgear r6300 asin b00em5ufp4 netgear smart wifi router ac1750 dual band gigabit r6300v2 the r6300 was installed at another location and has never given a lick of trouble i am on my second rma for the r6100 and rather than pay the 29 95 advance replacement fee i decided to scrap the router and apply the savings to the purchase of another router from a different company br br the r6100 was just as easy to set up as the r6300 just plug it in change the stock router settings to your own don't want to upset your neighbor who's stealing your wifi right and route unlike some i rather like the netgear genie application i didn't use it for set up but i like that i can graphically tell which devices are connected with purdy user assigned icons i suppose it has many other features you could use but i'm usually too busy using the thing to turn off the kid's wifi when i hear the wrong sounds coming from their tablets br br in fact my only complaint with the two r6100s i have owned has been the lan ports being a dinosaur i have a house that i wired with cat6 cable i therefore actually use the quaint things on the back that look like a big pots jack i really didn't mind the fact that although it says fast ethernet in the title of the item it's really only 10 100 or rather it would be if the jacks were properly soldered into the device on my two examples after about four months of use the entire block of 4 ethernet jacks on the device came loose and only intermittently connect as my voip and other devices depend on ethernet this is an unacceptable fault i've used strain relief devices hand cut pieces of styrofoam and just plain jiggling to get the things to stay connected they won't br br so i give up for now i'd try to go steal the r6300 back from my brother in law but then i'd have to talk to him

title: great sound but serious design flaw
text: i received my bose mini ii limted edition today the intent was to use it on the road as i am now and at home as a computer speaker i have a set of bose companion 20 speakers which are amazing but i want less wiring and more real estate on my desk at home br br of course i will use the bluetooth option occasionally but most of my applications will be with it hard wired to a computer or other source through the aux port br br right away i noticed a problem there is a horrible loud hum like a ground fault when it has any source connected to aux and the usb power source connected directly to the speaker or to the charging cradle br br i attempted various sources changing cables and power supplies other electrical outlets moving it around the problem persists br br incidentally my logitech bluetooth ue mini boom speakers and my bose companion 20's exhibit none of this connected to same source it is not being carried in on the input i tried multiple audio cables on multiple sources br br if you unplug either the source connected or the usb power source the hum stops br br then i went to google to research this problem many people have observed it with all the mini ii's but not the orginal mini br br bose has no fix it is a design flaw in the unit where they have not properly isolated the aux input from the power i read where some people found it went away if they powered charged it off the usb port on their computer the one i received won't do it it starts to charge and then stops the only option to use the aux input is to run it on battery only br br for my intended purpose this can't work the speaker is amazing in terms of sound quality from such a small unit but bose really messed up with this one br br i want to like it was tempted to try to live with it but the point was to end up with bose quality not a serious quirk one guy in a support thread said he undocks or slides the speaker off the cradle contacts a bit when he uses it direct connected to his computer br br there are too many other good products out there so i am returning this to amazon extremely disappointed

title: extreme convenience mediocre sound
text: these earbuds paired readily with my hp envy laptop running windows 10 and they almost instantaneously connect when i take them out of their charging case connecting to bluetooth on my laptop has been a bit hit or miss with other headphones so this is a big plus these also had no problem pairing with my moto e5 play and our amazon fire stick the bluetooth range is quite good keeping a solid connection even when i m dozens of feet from my computer the built in microphone is not great but the voice quality is adequate for phone calls and zoom i get a secure and reasonably comfortable fit with the medium silicone tips on these earbuds although having a size between small and medium would likely enhance the comfort further for me br br i m not impressed by the sound quality the treble is sometimes shrill and can be a bit sibilant on the highest notes bass is minimal and overall the sound is harsh i don t expect exceptional fidelity or a sense of the depth of the soundstage from budget earbuds but i much prefer the sound of a pair of 20 wired sony earbuds or 30 jlab bluetooth sport earbuds to these jvc buds i find myself reaching for the jvc gumy buds for zoom calls or streaming tv because they are convenient and unobtrusive but they just don t cut it for music if you re seeking inexpensive truly wireless earbuds for voice communication or for a kid who cares more about the form factor than sound quality these would fit the bill but there are nicer options out there in this price range if you can compromise on wires

title: a nice and unique case with some drawbacks
text: let's start off by saying that this is a nice quality case with respect to the materials used fit and finish for the most part paint packaging and even the tools provided i never owned an in win case and always liked the d frame so i picked this up when i had a chance to make a new machine based on what i have read and heard about in win products br br pros br beautiful paint br quality welds no sloppiness on any of them br motherboard cast base was perfect i read about others experiencing rough edges and some pitting br everything lined up and fit together well br all parts were labelled and grouped in an orderly fashion making assembly of the case go that much smoother br nice set of tools an tool case were included with the case br br cons br price the buy in cost of this in win case was pretty high br the rubber feet of the case came right off upon unboxing turns out the adhesive that attached the rubber to the br frame was completely dried out and would no longer hold makes me think how long this thing sat unsold in br warehouse br the tempered glass was slightly chipped on a rounded inner edge at first i thought user error but i am pretty meticulous br so i check my workbench area for any glass chips anything sparkling resembling a bit of glass and anything sharp br like glass nothing br next i do the same for the foam packaging of the case nothing br next i examine the box nothing br i'm stumped could not find anything that could lead me to believe i caused the blemish br i conclude it was maybe missed at quality control or occurred during transportation who knows br br for those interested in buying this case you will have to assemble it because it does arrive in pieces assembly is fairly straight forward but you must pay attention to the figures drawings in the instructions regarding orientation of how the parts fit together else you will be putting it together taking it apart then putting it together the correct way think ikea for the instructions br br i waited a while before posting a review of this case as to not let some of the negatives dominate that said i do like the case it is a nice show piece and conversation starter it's a welcome break from all the other computer boxes out there that i could have gotten the case would have gotten 5 stars had it not been for the rubber feet falling off and chipped glass br br hopefully the experience negative with my first in win case is isolated it will be at the top of my concerns when choosing a case for my next build and if considering another in win product

title: r i p macbook pro
text: after almost 10 years sticking with 15 34 macbook pro's and upgrading them every year or two next year i'll be seriously considering windows notebook options br br after giving this computer a couple of months my disappointment continues the big issues for me are br the keyboard is much worse for me than the older models or pretty much any normal notebook keyboard due to simply being too shallow and i'm sure anyone who types fast would experience a similar issue especially if you take a moment to actually compare your typing between it and a normal keyboard as you may not notice the subtle productivity downgrade in typing accuracy speed it brings even when it's there br the touchbar is literally something i've not one single time used in any way in which it did something for me which worked better than the old keys did and i miss the ways the old keys worked better than it pretty much every day for anyone used to remembering a keyboard shortcut or two the touchbar was the last feature we ever wanted while losing the row of physical keys is a big loss br the price for the high end specs i got it was around 1000 euros above what the relatively equivalent high end model was 3 years ago with my previous macbook pro that's really just too much to pay to get a new mac with updated internals especially when i would much prefer the old body with updated internals than this result of apple's 'innovation' br br i haven't mentioned the other what can only be described as anti user changes apple made the complete lack of usb ports sd card hdmi port etc when 99 of new hardware is still not using usb c to highlight how ridiculous this is even apple sell their own new iphone's with the old usb cable rather than the newer usb c thus requiring 100's more spent in adapters which then have to remembered to be taken with you everywhere and new costlier than ever charging cables the charging brick now comes both without the cable to the computer which you have to purchase separately and the extension cable and yet still costs more than ever i haven't mentioned these issues as the main issues because while these things are obviously inconvenient costly they simply haven't stuck out for me as a daily constant annoyance like having a keyboard that simply causes more mistakes and slower typing literally every minute i use the computer at least when not docked to a real keyboard and similarly the touchbar

title: not a bad value for your money but it could be better
text: the off brand led dvd combo tv in my mother in law's mil room recently bit the dust so i needed to find a replacement after doing some research this tv the supersonic sc 2412 24 inch 60hz led lit tv seemed to be the best compromise between value and performance there were tv s more highly rated but they cost about 1 3rd to more than this unit and the cheaper units were savaged in their reviews so i purchased and installed this tv and hoped for the best below are my impressions br br the good br easy to assemble and has a sufficient number of ports and some interesting features br the picture is great br includes a full featured remote although the buttons and descriptions are small which bothers the mil br br the not so good br the picture is great only when you re looking at the screen from a fairly direct ie head on angle br it does not include the ability to choose from several screen sizes such as normal zoom automatic etc it used to be that only the cheapest sets didn t allow you to select a screen size i was disappointed to see that this is not an option for this tv and the mil has complained about it br dvd operations are slow br br the bad br i expect speakers on led tvs to be fairly mediocre but these are worse than average both in their tinny sound and because the speakers just aren t very loud even when turned to maximum this is another complaint from the mil br the product description says that the tv is noise reducing which i take to mean that it levels out the sounds loud sounds either from sudden volume shifts during your show or for commercials if true it fails this test because the only time the tv comes close to being loud enough is when a commercial blares out of your speakers br br the neither good nor bad br the set is so uncommon that i wasn t able to get my comcast cable remote to recognize it so my mother in law has to use two remotes to operate the tv that's not this tv's fault but it is annoying br br overall this is an ok tv it doesn t have any fatal flaws although poor sound and volume come close or any outstanding features the picture quality might qualify here if you weren t forced into a somewhat direct viewing angle i ll be honest in that i was hoping for better but it s not a bad value for your money but you may want to consider spending more for a better set unless you want to listen to your mil's complaints three stars

title: not bad a solid value
text: we purchased this product for trade show use and selected this model for several reasons its clean styling its reasonable price and its support for both photos and video the video proved unreliable for reasons we couldn't figure out in time some video files played back fine while others rendered with the exact same codec and settings and output as the exact same type failed to play at all photos worked fine though the transitions clearly needed some more horsepower behind them as they were far from smooth we used the simple cross fade option br br all that said the frames looked good and definitely delivered on ease of use and reliability for us being able to manage the content on a pc and simply throw a card into the screen and turn it on is ideal i don't want to have to do any content management on the playback devices the screen itself is also good colors are accurate the viewing angle is far wider than some i've seen and contrast and saturation are not artificially inflated as on some lcds br br i wish there were more digital frame options with clean lines and styling of the frame itself and i hope that nix considers throwing some faster components into future models of this frame to improve its core performance

title: i like everything but the too snug fit
text: this sleeve for the sony prs 650 is well designed i like the fact that it zips only on 2 sides the left side and 3 4 of the bottom part of the sleeve are permanently sealed i don't think this sleeve was meant to act as a book cover for the prs 650 however the design ensures that the prs 650 will remain securely inside the sleeve when the e reader is not in use minimizing the chance that the contents of the sleeve might accidentally slip out when the sleeve is not completely zippered br br the quality of manufacture is pretty good i feel reasonably confident that the sleeve won't fall apart on me despite my having to put it through the rigors of accommodating both the prs 650 and its optional book cover it appears that optimally this sleeve was meant to accommodate only the prs 650 and not the optional cover that can be purchased for it as an accessory i wish the manufacturer had added perhaps 1 8 of an inch to the height and width of the sleeve so that it could better accommodate both that would have been ideal for me br br however as this is a soft sleeve it is possible to stretch it a tad by initially forcing the zipper to close around the bulk of the e reader resting inside its optional cover so far it seems to be working for me though it still makes for a tighter fit than i would have preferred br br otherwise i think this zipper sleeve is a decent product for the price i paid which was under 16 i can't say that i'm in love with this product i wanted to be but overall i will admit to liking it

title: some great things and some annoying things about this tablet we've had them for almost a year
text: there are a few great things about this tablet and a few annoying things br br great things price 2 year warranty even if your child breaks or spills anything on it children's user face is great except for one peeve i will explain below it comes with the hard case which has saved my tablets from destruction thrown down the stairs multiple times and not a scratch or break and worked fine afterward br br things you may need br first off you will need an sd card if you want movies downloaded to the tablet so your child has access to entertainment while offline not connected to the internet i have 32gb sd card and that's been more than enough for movies each movie is about 1 2gb or less of space with 8 gb of usable internal storage on the tablet itself it's seriously not enough a few apps like netflix and sling and maybe a few game apps will get you maxed out br br i purchased a carrying case for my tablets it fits the chargers and tablet nicely i purchased this one https www amazon com gp product b010cc9cnq ref oh aui detailpage o01 s01 ie utf8 psc 1 br br i also purchased headphones for my kids which kind of turned out to be useless because my kids pretty much refuse to use them that being said the sound is good on the tablet if they don't use headphones but not so great for my sanity hearing 2 tablets when traveling if you are interested in the headphones i purchased these and they are great when they are used however they will not fit in the carrying case i recommended above br https www amazon com gp product b01a5ro1s4 ref oh aui detailpage o01 s01 ie utf8 psc 1 br br now for the annoying stuff that i hope they resolve and i would seriously spend the money on new ones if they resolved these issues br br when you first get the tablet it's loaded with apps that you will need to remove access to if you do not want your child to have access to it i'm talking a couple hundred apps you will need to deselect otherwise if you hand over the tablet before doing this your child can download the app just by tapping their finger on it annoying the problem with this is that you aren't exactly sure which apps are great or not great so you'll find yourself spending countless hours researching the apps trying to figure out what's best because you don't want to deselect a good one so my first round of doing this i left a bunch of apps at their leisure to download if they liked it bad idea br my kids will tap the app and it will begin downloading they don't understand this so then they think it's not working so they tap another app and that one starts to download and the same thing happens before you know it they are downloading 10 apps all at once and then bugging you to fix it because none are opening and the tablet is now busy downloading all of them br br this problem caused me to restrict more apps they have access to download which was fine until they used up about 22gb of space on the sd card with apps and the rest being movies i put on the card when this occurs another annoying problem is on your hands the tablet gives an error stating that the storage is dangerously low and this causes the tablet to be unable to operate any apps or play movies so now you have to uninstall the bazillion apps they downloaded on to the sd card and the internal tablet to get it to function and that takes forever i gave up uninstalling multiple times and just wiped my sd cards and started over with re downloading the movies to it which also takes a lot of time but less time than uninstalling a bazillion apps br so from now on i have restricted access to their ability to download any apps at all unless i do it myself i'm so tired of maintaining storage br br i would love it if the kindle was like a cell phone with the apps only having the standard apps on it and then any other apps you choose to have you search and download it if you're anything like me you like word of mouth with apps and will download the apps you know are good because other parents have given the recommendation with that said i have the usual apps like netflix and recommend pbs kids app because it will play episodes from pbs cartoons like daniel tiger there is a nickelodeon one with their cartoon episodes there are plenty of educational apps too but i recommend researching them and letting your child tell you which one appeals to them br br lastly the chargers were crap they slip out easily and wiggle therefore does not charge the kindle we have had to sit there and get the charger to stay in place correctly so that it charges we called amazon and they offered us new chargers for free which i just process and hope that it works if it does not and it's the tablet we will have to call them again and have the tablets fixed or replaced under the warranty

title: gs316 gets a d but netgear gets an f
text: this review is about the netgear 16 port gigabit ethernet switch model gs316 which i bought to replace my 10 year old 3com gigabit switch which was beginning to show its age br br initially i was impressed it was compact and solidly built it negotiated with my various computers very quickly 1 to 5 seconds and transferred data at over 100 mbytes per second its temperature was never warmer than ambient roughly 65 degrees f it worked fine for 32 days br br one morning i could not connect with my e mail after trying to ping the server then other computers on my lan then my modem router i went to investigate i found that the switch had power the power led was green but the port indicators were dark on have the ports and the other half indicated gigabit connections even with no devices connected i tried cycling the power no change i disconnected all the ethernet cables and cycled the power again no change i disconnected the power for 5 minutes and reconnected no change the switch was dead i reinstalled my old 3com switch so that i had a working network everything worked fine br br because i had registered it with netgear tried to submit a warranty claim on the web site but they wanted proof of purchase in the form of an image i was unclear what they wanted so i contacted amazon to see if they had some form that would satisfy this requirement this was best thing about this experience because the technical folks at amazon were amazing i explained my problem to millena in the amazon chat room and she contacted netgear for me after about an hour of waiting we finally got someone at netgear millena was going to call me and conference the netgear technician into the call netgear declined to participate in a conference call because of privacy concerns so millena connected me to netgear and dropped out of the call but we stayed in the online chat br br after i described in detail the steps i had taken and the state of the switch the netgear technician insisted i walk through her script which included disconnecting my 3com switch severing my online chat with millena and repeating all the steps i had already told her i had done finally satisfied that the switch was dead she offered to create an rma and gave me three options 1 send the dead switch to them at my expense and when they received it they would send me a replacement 2 give them 16 90 to send me a replacement by ground transportation with a return shipping label or 3 give them 29 90 to send me a replacement by air with a return shipping label so much for their warranty br br i contacted amazon again this time i chatted with chris after i explained the netgear rma options chris offered to refunded my purchase and give me a return shipping label i took the offer and replaced the netgear switch with a similar switch from cisco a little more expensive but its reviews were generally more positive nothing less than a 3 br br let me be clear new electronics sometimes fail and need to be replaced but the hoops that netgear required to make a warranty claim are unacceptable i device that fails within 90 days should be replaced expeditiously for free i give the switch a d and netgear gets an f

title: space conscious but odd
text: this wireless keyboard is a good solution for those who want to take up less space on a desk but require a keyboard for their desktop or want an external keyboard for their laptop it is a bit awkward to get used to as it is a smaller size than a typical full keyboard the board itself is lightweight and easy to store or travel with there are stands at the back that can be flipped up or down to add an angle to the keyboard while typing br br the range of the wireless dongle usb to the keyboard is good and i have not had any synching errors while using the keyboard the keys have not stuck when i used them and the typing noise is quieter than other keyboards i have used the sound is more hollow than other basic keyboards i have used and much different than a mechanical keyboard the arrow keys are a bit awkward and take some time to get used to they are located on the bottom right and are small and tight together they also double for other uses when you hold the fn key down br br all in all this is a decent keyboard that works as intended while taking up less space than the average keyboard it has a nice decorative top and all the keys function without sticking or causing errors in your typing it does take some getting used to as the keys have a hollow like sound and the arrow keys are a bit difficult to use at first however if you are looking for a keyboard that is smaller and can easily travel this keyboard would be a good purchase

text: buffalo tech support has been hit or miss my first linkstation pro was replaced after getting stuck in emergency mode by amazon with a new one and they are great about this now my second one froze up while flashing the firmware buffalo tells me it's hosed dude one guy tells me to remove the drive connect it directly to the computer to try to retrieve the data another tells me this is unsupported and voids the warranty a third told me to run tftp boot which seems to see the drive but then nas nav cannot find it to force the update so a simple firmware update has become a ridiculously time consuming mess connecting to the computer the drive shows up as empty and cannot be assigned a letter due to the gtp partition being apparently impenetrable then buffalo says they will replace my unit with a refurbished unit with an advanced rma and i should try the old drive in the new chassis ok but they neglected to tell me i was being charged an extra 30 for one last unlikely troubleshooting step amazon is willing to give me a new unit in advance for free but buffalo gives me a refurbished one for 30 extra which i have less confidence in than a new one this makes no sense in addition to the completely inconsistent tech support a nas should be simple and secure yet they take no responsibility for losing all my data through a failed update and have me go through steps beyond what the average customer cares to understand and or try in other words neither simple nor secure if a simple update can erase all data why was i not warned to back up my back up with so many bugs you'd think they'd provide an on line backup service so i don't need additional devices and to give peace of mind using the product at least make an effort not to leave customers out in the cold

title: decent remote hardware software needs work
text: after considering several urc models r50 and r40 i decided to try the harmony 700 here's my take br pros br excellent button backlighting very readable white automatically comes on when remote is picked up why aren't the activity hard buttons backlit br very good button layout buttons are easy to reach and operate br nice overall size not too big not too small buttons are not too small to easily operate br buttons feel great very responsive br database seems comprehensive with many discrete codes excellent br easy to add devices using the device model number br br cons br overall physical shape and balance is poor uses two aa batteries located near the end the remote is smooth and slippery in hand some simple texturing scallops or other features on the underside would help greatly maybe i need some board tape to put underneath add two more aa batteries and the remote would feel much better balanced too br br programming is wizard style and not very flexible i want more control over how the remote operates my system logitech really locks the user into setting the remote up as they would have it be which isn't ideal in some circumstances br br programming software is mediocre the online software myharmony com is a different account than the downloaded software 7 7 0 each has their own account the online software is much easier to use but does not contain all the features of the downloadable software if you set it up with one you can't edit with the other you have to set it up again in the other program one of my computers win7 up to date latest drivers ie and mozilla browsers etc would not load either software solution and tech support was useless i had to use my older vista machine to program not sure what i would have done without it probably returned the remote using either software package is akin to using turbotax for your taxes although much less sophisticated yet it's very dumbed down which could be good for some people br br speaking of software here's a big negative i had to use 7 7 0 which only allows me to add 6 devices to the remote even though they advertise 8 devices ends up you must use myharmony com to get 8 devices what if myharmony com doesn't allow me to program the remote correctly for my system i feel cheated 2 devices which i need br br back to software you must have internet for either software solution to work yikes my cell is my internet makes for a pain to program br br learning commands has been mostly a miss software seems to lock up and i must logout login to do anything learned command is not frustrating and nearly useless luckily the databases are good but not 100 complete working on a work around now br br can't edit an activity to perform exactly how i'd like i want complete ability to edit every step in the activity i can work around it by adding several other steps at the end of the predefined activity commands but why should i have to let me edit the activity and fix the problems in the first place br br conclusion br the remote seems durable enough once it's up and running and properly programmed it works well activities work better than i expected i don't see any issues with ir power but i use an ir repeater blaster for my cabinet the lcd is just ok nothing to write home about in quality and readability off angle i will be keeping the remote although i'd really like my 2 other devices available in the 7 7 0 software not holding my breathe i get the impression that logitech doesn't care much about the harmony remote product line and supporting it based on the software woes and tech support haha what a joke i will most likely get a urc r40 in the near future and use the harmony to learn the commands to it as needed as mentioned the harmony has a great command database with discretes based on my experience the remote is overpriced seems like it's worth 50

title: be careful with product description
text: here are the things i've noted br br speaker brackets are not described on the page they must use existing or newly drilled bolts for mounting the shark speaker handle bar conversions cause them to be mounted sideways on your handlebars i ended up making these look nice by mounting with windshield bolts down near the gas tank br br 250w power the amp may be but each of your speakers are only 50w there is a port for a subwoofer but the manual says it only handles 60w how you are supposed to use 250w with this setup is beyond me without buying larger speakers and throwing away the ones that come with it br br speakers may be waterproof i have my doubts but the amplifier is not this amplifier makes no sense for motorcycles the usb and sd card slots are open enough you can see circuit boards inside the amp the huge yeah right light up display can only be viewed from an angle this means you have to mount the amp on the front of the bike with the card slots facing up yeah rain when it is not waterproof if you want to be able to control the radio in addition the amplifier mounting holes do not make sense for a motorcycle the amount of flat area required to use the mounting holes does not exist on any bike i've ever seen i ended up using a windshield bolt drilling a new hole in the amp and breaking off one of the mounting brackets to make it fit now i have electrical tape covering all the openings in the amp in case of rain br br cables the speaker cables will go from one end of the bike to another the amp power cable barely made it from the front to the battery it took some creative routing and removing the seats and gas tank br br instructions are non existent forget about it don't even open them there are no installation instructions and the use instructions are incorrect and do not even include the remote br br remote has to be pointed straight at amp ir port worthless if you hide the amp br br for all of this criticism the system sounds good installed in a few hours and works we'll see for how long br br i gave this 3 stars because if you have the time and creativity you can make it work and sound good

title: better than the traditional dealer audio offerings
text: our 12 year old car was ready to be retired although it was showing its age the sound system in our car had held up surprisingly well over the years however for our new car we wanted to do something different for the sound system instead of going with one of the manufacturer's standard packages we decided to roll our own car audio system br br the new system is based around the planet audio psa4cb amplifier and controller we found the psa4cb to be more versatile than the traditional options offered by the dealer the ability to have a remote control and bluetooth access was especially appealing the installation instructions were quite good every cable and there are quite a few of them is illustrated and defined in the documentation some but not all cables are label on the amp it would have been better if all cables had been labeled br br i am an electrical engineer and a former broadcast engineer so installation wasn't very hard for me to accomplish the biggest issue was finding a place to mount the psa4cb given today's limited dash space and the relatively short cables provided with the amp for most people however i would recommend to have the psa4cb professionally installed unless you are electrically and mechanically inclined this will save you from any heartache or grief br br despite some nits discussed above i found the planet audio psa4cb is really a well designed and thought out product well done planet audio
